code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test30-SEP-2016Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, suggesting impaired renal function. Urinalysis reveals significant proteinuria and glucosuria, with low specific gravity and abnormal urine appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal odor), but no hematuria or leukocyturia. These findings are consistent with possible chronic kidney disease and renal glycosuria; urinary tract infection is less likely. Further nephrological evaluation is recommended.
